title: Definitive anti-dumping duties imposed for fabrics ( classified in headings 5407, 5513-4-5-6) originating/consigned from certain EU countries
bodyHtml:
The anti-dumping investigations against imports of "synthetic filament yarn woven fabrics (for clothing)" classified under customs tariff position 54.07 and "woven fabrics made of synthetic or artificial continuous fibers" classified in customs tariff positions 55.13, 55.14, 55.15, 55.16, originating/consigned from certain EU countries, have been completed.

As a result of the investigation, it has been decided to continue the application of the anti-dumping measures on the import of fabrics in tariff positions 5407, 5513, 5514, 5515, and 5516 from Italy, Spain, and Germany, which are currently in force, by modifying them in accordance with the findings of the investigation.

Additionally, anti-dumping measures will be imposed on the import of fabrics in tariff positions 5407, 5513, 5514, 5515, and 5516 from Austria, Belgium, the Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Croatia, the Netherlands, Ireland, Sweden, Latvia, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Hungary, Malta, Portugal, Romania, Slovakia, and Slovenia.

The anti-dumping measures will be applied as follows:

For fabrics in tariff position 5407 used for clothing: 21.13 percent and 42.44 percent of CIF value.

For "woven fabrics made of synthetic or artificial continuous fibers" in tariff positions 5513-5514-5515-5516; 44 percent of CIF value.

These anti-dumping measures have come into effect today.

Certain producers in Italy, Germany, Spain, and the Czech Republic have been exempted from the measures.

To determine the specific amounts of anti-dumping measures to be applied based on the Harmonized System Codes (GTIP) and companies, it is necessary to refer to Tables 1 and 2 of the Regulation and, for GTIPs subject to measures under tariff position 54.07, to Annex 1 of the Regulation.

For more information on the Regulation on Preventing Unfair Competition in Imports (No: 2024/8), click here.
